page: Amazon Web Services m8i.24xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance across multiple domains. The memory subsystem achieves top-tier read-only bandwidth for both small and large blocks, alongside strong write performance. In computational tests, the server ranks in the top tier for Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores, as well as Passmark CPU tests, including extended instructions, encryption, and physics. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and software compilation also register top-tier results. For database workloads, the instance demonstrates top-tier performance in Redis SET operations and Passmark database operations.

The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which accelerate cryptographic and matrix math operations. In LLM inference benchmarks, the instance achieves top-tier prompt processing speeds across small, medium, and large models, though text generation speeds range from average to strong. Given its balanced resource allocation and high memory bandwidth, the m8i.24xlarge is well-suited for enterprise applications, database hosting, software compilation pipelines, and medium-scale machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i.24xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs (48 physical cores) operating at 3.9 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory. While the instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, memory read bandwidth, software compilation, and database operations. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which significantly accelerate cryptographic tasks and matrix math. This resource profile makes the m8i.24xlarge highly efficient for demanding enterprise workloads, including database management, multi-threaded software compilation, and large language model prompt processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.24xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 40 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier database and compute performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.24xlarge is a general-purpose server with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-density general-purpose server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed DDR5 memory for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs running at 3.9 GHz
384 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier memory read bandwidth and database performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction set support
Optimized for database operations, software compilation, and LLM prompt processing
